channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: DCI Apprehends Two Governor Natembeya’s Staff in Trans Nzoia for Organised Crime
item.description: In Trans Nzoia County, the Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) has arrested two individuals linked to Governor George Natembeya’s office for alleged involvement in organised criminal activiti...

item.title: Solo Dining Grows in Popularity Worldwide as Data Shows Higher Spending, Despite Occasional Restaurant Resistance
item.description: Solo dining is experiencing a significant global surge in popularity, particularly among travelers and in major business hubs, according to data from OpenTable. The reservation platform reported a 19%...

item.title: California officials capture and release a bear at Hansen Dam after it wandered near people
item.description: California Department of Fish and Wildlife officials captured a 275-pound bear at Hansen Dam on Monday after it had caused concern among local residents over the weekend. The black bear was first spot...

item.title: Kenya Advises Citizens in South Africa to Stay Safe Amid Rising Violence
item.description: The Kenyan government don issue warning to all its citizens wey dey South Africa due to the rise in xenophobic attacks. The Kenya High Commission for Pretoria tell Kenyans make dem avoid areas wey pro...

item.title: Wu Yize Claims World Snooker Championship in First Deciding Frame Since 2002
item.description: Wu Yize has made history by becoming the second-youngest world snooker champion at the age of 22, defeating Shaun Murphy 18-17 in an extraordinary final at the Crucible Theatre. This match marked the ...

item.title: Guardiola and Haaland Respond After Man City Drop Points Against Everton
item.description: Manchester City suffered a major setback in the Premier League title race after being held to a 3-3 draw by Everton at the Hill Dickinson Stadium. The result handed Arsenal the advantage, as they now ...

item.title: Prosecution outlines allegations in Gold Coast drugged smoothie murder trial
item.description: In the Supreme Court on the Gold Coast, Maree Mavis Crabtree, 59, faces charges of murdering her 26-year-old son, Jonathan Crabtree, after allegedly giving him a smoothie spiked with oxycodone in July...

item.title: State and institutions must act decisively on immigration and law enforcement
item.description: South Africa, like many countries worldwide, is facing challenges with undocumented immigration and porous borders. While some blame immigrants for taking jobs or overburdening public services, the ar...

item.title: Inquiry Confirms Major Mismanagement in Karachi Matric Exams with 170 Centres Changed
item.description: KARACHI: A detailed inquiry into the Board of Secondary Education Karachi (BSEK) matric exams has revealed serious irregularities, including the shifting of over 170 examination centres during the exa...

item.title: Alberta authorities respond to voter information breach amid ongoing investigations
item.description: Premier Danielle Smith addressed the Alberta legislature regarding a recent breach of the provincial voter database, marking her first in-person update beyond a social media statement. Millions of Alb...

item.title: Eric Celiz posts bail in Pasay court over alleged false news on President Marcos' health
item.description: Social media personality Eric Celiz posted a bail amounting to P36,000 at a Pasay court on Tuesday in connection with charges alleging that he spread false information regarding President Ferdinand Ma...

item.title: CCM Encourages Youth to Promote Peace and National Unity
item.description: Dodoma — The ruling party Chama Cha Mapinduzi (CCM) has made a strong call to young educated Tanzanians to actively protect peace and resist political chaos, which the party says is being promoted by ...

item.title: Driver receives £8,500 in ULEZ fines due to cloned number plate
item.description: A motorist from Maidenhead has been landed with £8,500 in unpaid ULEZ penalty charge notices (PCNs) after criminals cloned his car's number plate. The victim, referred to as RJ, has received 77 PCNs f...

item.title: DCI Investigates Possible Poisoning in Death of Veteran Doctor Job Obwaka
item.description: Detectives from the Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) in Kenya have expanded their probe into the sudden death of renowned obstetrician and gynaecologist Dr. Job Obwaka, now looking at susp...

item.title: HSBC takes a cautious step into private credit after setting aside funds for alleged UK fraud-related losses
item.description: HSBC has made a tentative move into the private‑credit market after allocating a reserve to cover potential losses linked to an alleged fraud in private markets in the UK, according to reporting from ...

item.title: UBA arrests three influencers for spreading false divorce rumours about Tony Elumelu
item.description: Na wah o! Three social media influencers don land for trouble as dem dey arrested for dey spread false story say billionaire and UBA Chairman, Tony Elumelu, don divorce him wife, Awele. United Bank fo...

item.title: Family of man wrongly accused of 1997 murder seeks justice and compensation
item.description: The widow of Sougat Mukherjee, an Indian businessman falsely accused of a 1997 murder in Glasgow, has appealed to Prime Minister Narendra Modi for support in suing the Scottish authorities. Sougat, wh...

item.title: BJP to Form Government in West Bengal, Swearing-in Scheduled for May 9 on Tagore's Birth Anniversary
item.description: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) has emerged victorious in the recent West Bengal Assembly elections, securing the mandate to form the state government. The party's government is scheduled to take oat...

item.title: Manitoba Speaker ejects PC MLA for one day over remarks about premier and tightens debate rules
item.description: A Progressive Conservative member of Manitoba’s legislature was suspended for one day after making remarks about Premier Wab Kinew that the Speaker deemed inappropriate. Speaker Tom Lindsey ordered ML...

item.title: NCCIA arrests 13 individuals in Punjab over social media posts against state
item.description: LAHORE: The National Cyber Crime Investigation Agency (NCCIA) has arrested 13 social media activists from different cities in Punjab over alleged anti-state activities, sources confirmed on Tuesday. T...
